qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in Image Processing Endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Shares the GET /images endpoint with the advisory, but the root cause is entirely different. The finding describes missing URL validation allowing external fetch (SSRF), while the advisory describes unbounded SVG use-element shadow tree and pattern tile dimension processing causing resource exhaustion (DoS). Different vulnerable code path, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Password Reset Token Exposed in API Response
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Completely different endpoint (POST /customers/reset-password), root cause (token returned in response body), and vulnerability class (information disclosure/account takeover) from the advisory's DoS via SVG processing on GET /images.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL Injection in Product Deletion Event Subscriber
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different endpoint (DELETE /products/:id), different root cause (unsafe string interpolation in SQL), different vulnerability class (SQL injection), and requires authentication (admin). No overlap with the advisory's unauthenticated DoS via SVG processing on GET /images.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Weak Default Session Cookie Secret
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause (hardcoded default cookie secret 'keyboard cat'), different vulnerability class (session forgery/authentication bypass), and different affected component (authentication module). No overlap with the advisory's DoS via SVG processing on GET /images.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a DoS vulnerability where SVG processing on GET /images fails to limit use-element shadow tree height or pattern tile dimensions, causing unbounded resource consumption (CWE-1050). Finding vuln-001 shares the GET /images endpoint but describes SSRF via external URL fetching—a completely different root cause and vulnerability class. The other three findings (password reset token exposure, SQL injection, default cookie secret) have no overlap with the advisory whatsoever.
evidence: Advisory root cause: unbounded SVG use-element shadow tree and pattern tile dimension processing on GET /images causing resource exhaustion
vuln-001 root cause: missing URL validation allowing fetch() of arbitrary external URLs on GET /images (SSRF, not DoS)
vuln-002 root cause: password reset token returned in API response body (information disclosure, different endpoint)
vuln-003 root cause: unsafe string interpolation in SQL DELETE query via event subscriber (SQL injection, different endpoint, requires auth)
vuln-004 root cause: hardcoded default cookie secret 'keyboard cat' (session forgery, different component)
